name: Zerodha Diwali Load Test — Gatling & k6
scenario: Zerodha is preparing for the Diwali trading surge. Last year the trading API crashed at 50K concurrent users. Write a Gatling load test, stress test, and performance regression gate to prevent a repeat.

core.description: PaymentLoadSimulation Gatling class: extends Simulation. HTTP protocol: baseUrl localhost:8080, JSON headers. Load injection: atOnceUsers(10) + rampUsers(100) over 30s.
k6 script (load-test.js): options with vus=100, duration=30s, thresholds p(99)<500 and http_req_failed rate<0.01. Default function calling GET /api/portfolio.
PerformanceRegressionChecker @Service: isAcceptable(baseline, result) with 10% p99 tolerance. reportSummary returns PASS/FAIL string.
@SpringBootTest (3 tests): no-regression passes, 25% p99 regression fails, growing heap (soak) detected. All 3 pass.
README: explains p99 vs average (why p99 is the right SLA metric), with Zerodha Diwali example.
Soak test detector: takes heap snapshots, returns true if heap grew > 150MB over last 3 measurements.

bonus.description: CSV feeder Gatling simulation: payments.csv with merchantId,amount,gateway. Each VU gets different row. .circular() to avoid running out of data.
TradeDataGenerator @Service: weighted symbol distribution (RELIANCE 25%), log-normal amount distribution. @SpringBootTest: RELIANCE most frequent, all amounts in valid range.
RegressionHistory @Service: stores last 5 p99 values (Deque), isRegressionTrend() returns true if last 3 strictly increasing. @SpringBootTest: trend detected, stable not detected.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. percentile3 in Gatling DSL = p99 (NOT percentile99!). This is Gatling's naming: percentile1=p50, percentile2=p75, percentile3=p99. k6 thresholds fail the run and return exit code 1 — this is what blocks CI, not the check() assertions (checks are informational only). Gatling { setUp(...) }: instance initializer block — the simulation setup code goes here, not in a main method. 10% regression tolerance: maxAllowedP99 = baseline.p99 * 1.10 (round up). Never test against production database — always use staging with production-like data. Soak test memory leak: compare snapshots[last] - snapshots[0] for overall trend, not just consecutive difference (reduces GC noise impact).
